name: Narrator / Lawyer
role: Elderly Wall Street lawyer who employs Bartleby, Turkey, Nippers, and Ginger Nut and narrates the crisis.

name: Bartleby
role: Pallid scrivener hired by the narrator who gradually refuses work, refuses to leave, and dies in the Tombs.

name: Turkey
role: Older copyist employed by the narrator, productive in mornings and volatile after noon.

name: Nippers
role: Young copyist employed by the narrator, irritable in mornings and ambitious beyond copying.

name: Ginger Nut
role: Twelve-year-old office boy, errand runner, cleaner, and student at law in the narrator’s office.

name: Ginger Nut’s Father
role: Carman who sends Ginger Nut to the narrator’s office to rise beyond cart work.

name: John Jacob Astor
role: Wealthy former client/employer whose good opinion confirms the narrator’s professional respectability.

name: The Cleaning Woman
role: Woman residing in the attic who sweeps, dusts, scrubs the office, and keeps one key.

name: Nippers’s Clients
role: Ambiguous visitors whom Nippers calls clients, including at least one likely creditor.

name: Custom House and Post Office Vendors
role: Nearby vendors from whom Ginger Nut buys apples and ginger cakes for the copyists.

name: Professional Friends
role: The narrator’s legal acquaintances whose remarks about Bartleby pressure him to act.

name: Visiting Attorney
role: Attorney who visits the office and attempts to get information from Bartleby.

name: Legal Gentleman
role: Lawyer present during a Reference who asks idle Bartleby to fetch papers.

name: Lawyers and Witnesses
role: Professional participants who crowd the office during a Reference and observe Bartleby’s refusal.

name: Mr. B / New Lawyer Tenant
role: Lawyer who takes the narrator’s old office and complains that Bartleby refuses to leave.

name: Landlord of No. Wall Street
role: Landlord who pressures the narrator about Bartleby and has him removed to the Tombs.

name: Other Tenants
role: Tenants in the Wall Street building disturbed by Bartleby’s presence in the entry and stairs.

name: Police / Constables
role: Authorities called by the landlord to remove Bartleby as a vagrant.

name: Tombs Officer
role: Official at the Halls of Justice who confirms Bartleby is confined and allows an interview.

name: The Turnkeys
role: Prison keepers who confirm the grub-man’s role and later direct the narrator to Bartleby.

name: The Grub-Man
role: Prison food provider whom the narrator pays to give Bartleby better meals.

name: Monroe Edwards
role: Former prisoner mentioned by the grub-man as someone who died of consumption at Sing-Sing.

name: Dead Letter Office
role: Former workplace where rumor says Bartleby served as a subordinate clerk before removal.

name: The Administration
role: Government administration whose change allegedly removed Bartleby from the Dead Letter Office.
